title: anodendron coriaceum
intro: Anodendron coriaceum, also known as the 'devil's walking stick', is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-bacterial properties, which can help alleviate various health issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the Anodendron coriaceum plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
